scenarios.title: Default editor
setup: Use JSON as active version type.
checkpoints: macOS traffic lights do not collide with tabs.
Active tab is soft and visually connected with the editor area.
Inactive tab text is lighter than active tab text.
Version rail aligns with the top of the editor column.
Line numbers and editor text keep the expected compact spacing.
Toolbar labels and icons stay quiet and readable.

scenarios.title: Notes menu open
setup: Click the Notes control in the top bar.
Keep the active project visible in the menu.
checkpoints: Menu uses the same warm white surface and rounded corners as the app.
Active project row is visible without feeling heavy.
New Project action is separated but not visually loud.
Menu shadow is soft and does not look like a system default popup.

scenarios.title: Project context menu
setup: Keep Rename Project, Close Tab, and Delete Project visible.
checkpoints: Context menu matches LightEdit surface style.
Danger action is separated and lower emphasis until hovered.
No WebView default Reload or Inspect Element menu appears.

scenarios.title: Long name tooltip
setup: Rename a project or version to a long name.
Hover the truncated label for about 0.8s.
checkpoints: Tooltip uses the custom LightEdit style instead of a native gray title bubble.
Tooltip appears quickly enough for daily use but not instantly on pass-through hover.
Tooltip does not cover the close button or primary label.
